questions.question: What is the primary function of a neighborhood operator in image processing?
options: A) To apply a complex algorithm to the entire image
B) To perform a local operation on a small region of the image
C) To apply a filter to the entire image
D) To perform an edge detection operation
questions.explanation: Neighborhood operators are used to perform local operations on small regions of the image.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a 2D DCT and a 2D IDCT?
options: A) The 2D DCT is used for image compression, while the 2D IDCT is used for image reconstruction
B) The 2D DCT is used for image reconstruction, while the 2D IDCT is used for image compression
C) The 2D DCT is used for image filtering, while the 2D IDCT is used for image sharpening
D) The 2D DCT is used for image sharpening, while the 2D IDCT is used for image filtering
questions.explanation: The 2D DCT is used for image compression, while the 2D IDCT is used for image reconstruction.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using the wavelet transform in image processing?
options: A) It allows for more efficient compression of images
B) It allows for more efficient reconstruction of images
C) It allows for more efficient filtering of images
D) It allows for more efficient feature extraction from images
questions.explanation: The wavelet transform allows for more efficient feature extraction from images.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a pyramid in image processing?
options: A) To apply a complex algorithm to the entire image
B) To perform a local operation on a small region of the image
C) To repeatedly apply a small filter to the entire image
D) To perform a hierarchical representation of the image
questions.explanation: The pyramid is used to perform a hierarchical representation of the image.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a low-pass filter and a high-pass filter?
options: A) A low-pass filter preserves the high frequency components of the image, while a high-pass filter preserves the low frequency components
B) A low-pass filter preserves the low frequency components of the image, while a high-pass filter preserves the high frequency components
C) A low-pass filter is used for image sharpening, while a high-pass filter is used for image blurring
D) A low-pass filter is used for image blurring, while a high-pass filter is used for image sharpening
questions.explanation: A low-pass filter preserves the low frequency components of the image, while a high-pass filter preserves the high frequency components.
